paper.title_en: Pressure-induced PT Symmetry Breaking in LaMnSi
paper.abstract_en: We investigate the magnetotransport properties of the antiferromagnetic metal LaMnSi, in which the collinear magnetic order breaks both spatial inversion (P) and time-reversal (T) symmetry yet preserves their combined PT symmetry. High pressure is found to suppress this PT-symmetric antiferromagnetic phase, inducing a transition into a PT-broken state characterized by a large anomalous Hall effect. Based on symmetry analysis, we propose a candidate magnetic structure for the high-pressure phase. Subsequent band calculations for this structure reveal the emergence of band splitting and orbital-dependent spin polarization. Our results establish LaMnSi as an ideal platform for controlling PT symmetry breaking via external parameters.
